  1. Junji Ito

    Japanese horror manga artist
    OverviewMangaAdaptationsThemes
    howtolovecomics.com
    See More

    Overview

    Junji Ito is a Japanese horror manga artist renowned for works like Tomie, Uzumaki, and Gyo, blending body horror, cosmic dread, and surreal imagery. Initially a dental technician, he debuted in 1987 with Tomie, winning the Kazuo Umezu Prize. Influenced by H.P. Lovecraft, Kazuo Umezu, and Shigeru Mizuki, his Eisner Award-winning works have inspired global adaptations and cemented his status as a modern horror icon.

    Age:
    62 years (Jul 31, 1963)
    Spouse:
    Ayako Ishiguro (2006–)

    artincontext.org
    Junji Ito - The Master of Horror Manga - artincontext.orgJunji Ito is a renowned Japanese manga artist celebrated for his unique contributions to the horror genre. With a career spanning over three decades, Ito’s work is characterized by its intricate line art, unsettling imagery, and deeply psychological narratives that explore the human condition and the macabre. His most famous works, including Uzumaki and Tomie, delve into themes of obsession, fear, and the supernatural, earning him a devoted international following.     Junji Ito - Wikipedia

    • Junji Ito (Japanese: 伊藤 潤二, Hepburn: Itō Junji; born July 31, 1963) is a Japanese horror manga artist. Some of his most notable works include Tomie, a series chronicling an immortal girl who drives her stricken admirers to madness; Uzumaki, a three-volume series about a town cursed by spirals; and Gyo, a two-volume story in which fish are controlled by a strain of bacteria called "the deat…
